title: Primary cortical neuron nucleofection Amaxa Lonza
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6i10g","text":"Protocol to nucleofect primary murine cortical neurons. Protocol used in Palumbos et al., 2025","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vpbe","text":"Protocol is adapted from protocol provided by LONZA- \"Amaxa mouse neuron nucleofector kit.\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"b8gbe","text":"Prepare INB nucleofection solution (ahead of time)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"i4ygx","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xn1yy","text":"Composition:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gfhce","text":"- 135 mM KCl: 13.5 mL of 1M KCl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d87ac","text":"- 0.2 mM CaCl2: 20 μL of 1M CaCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"uisex","text":"- 2 mM MgCl2: 200 μL of 1M MgCl2","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44lbf","text":"- 10 mM HEPES pH 7.3: 1 mL of 1M HEPES","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"btwi5","text":"- 5 mM EGTA: 1 mL of 0.5M EGTA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0ojcl","text":"- 84.28 mL ddH2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsus9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"royf5","text":"Total: 100 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make INB buffer Composition Volume of Stock 1 135 mM KCl 13.5 mL 1M KCl 2 0.2 mM CaCl2 20 μL 1M CaCl2 3 2 mM MgCl2 200 μL 1M MgCl2 4 10 mM HEPES pH 7.3 1 mL 1M HEPES 5 5 mM EGTA 1 mL 0.5M EGTA 6 84.28 mL ddH2O 7 Total 100mL
Follow previously published protocol to isolate cortical neurons from e15.5 mice.
Determine concentration of neurons.
Collect 3 million neurons in a 15 mL conical tube for each nucleofection.
Pellet cells at 1.1k RPM for 5 minutes.
Prepare 100 µL of INB per nucleofection.
Add appropriate amount of DNA.
For shRNA, add 3 µg DNA/million neurons.
For reporters, add 1 µg DNA/million neurons.
Remove remaining attachment media using P1000 and P200 (sequential to get total volume without perturbing pellet).
Resuspend pelleted neurons in 100 µL of INB + DNA.
Mix using P200. 2X up and down should be sufficient- you do not want to be too harsh
Transfer cell suspension to nucleofection cuvette.
Start Amaxa Nucleofector II Device (VPG-1001) and load experiment program 0-005.
Ensure that cell suspension covers the bottom of the cuvette.
Place nucleofection cuvette with closed lid into retainer of nucleofector.
Groove containing solution should align with indents.
Nucleofect with program 0-005 by pressing “Start”.
Carefully remove cuvette.
Immediately add pre-warmed media to nucleofected neurons.
For 3 million cells, add 200 µL so total volume is 300 µL.
Plate desired amount of cells. Nucleofected neurons take longer to attach. Wait 24hours before changing from attachment to maintenance media
For 10-cm dish, add 5 million.
For imaging dish, add 500K.
Aliquot into 500µL Aliquots should not be refrozen Store at -80
creator: Sierra Palumbos
